rationale: The page provides a detailed overview of the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC), including its constitutional provisions, composition, tenure, removal process, independence safeguards, and functions. It also includes a brief section on the types of state consent required for CBI investigations.
cleanedText:
Consent of State for CBI
- General: CBI is not required to seek fresh permission every time it enters that state in connection with investigation for every case. Section 6 of DSPE Act empowers state government to give or deny consent to CBI officer.
- Specific: CBI would have to apply to state government in every case.
2.3. UNION PUBLIC SERVICE COMMISSION (UPSC)
Why in the news?
Recently, UPSC completed a century of its establishment on 1st October 2025.
UPSC
- Genesis: Central recruiting agency of India; Constitutional body under Articles 315-323 (Part XIV).
- Composition: Chairman and Members appointed by President of India; strength at President's discretion. At least 50% members must have 10 years of government service.
- Tenure: Six years or until they attain the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.
- Removal: Grounds include insolvency, external paid employment, physical/mental unfitness, and misbehaviour.
- In case of misbehaviour, President refers matter to Supreme Court, whose advice is binding.
- Misbehaviour includes holding interest in a government contract.
- Independence Safeguards:
- Service conditions cannot be altered to their disadvantage.
- Salaries charged on Consolidated Fund of India.
- Chairman barred from further government employment; Members eligible only as UPSC Chairperson or State PSC Chairperson.
- Functions:
- Conducts examinations for All-India Services, Central Services, and UT services.
- Assists states in joint recruitment on request.
- Reporting: Submits annual report to the President (Article 323), laid before Parliament with reasons for non-acceptance of advice, if any.
Note: For more details on Evolution of UPSC, refer to Appendix at the end of this document.
